recommendation.title: Bull Call Spread — Enter POST-Earnings, Stock Has Surpassed Most Analyst Targets
recommendation.entryNote: ⚠️ EARNINGS MAY 28 — mandatory wait. IV will inflate into the print and crush post-announcement. Stock is at $164.74, already trading ABOVE the average analyst price target of $120 — the April surge (+67%) has outrun most sell-side models. The spread contains cost in a high-IV environment (~55–65% estimated). If earnings confirm the AI ASIC thesis and analysts raise targets to $170–200 range, stock has room to $185+. If earnings disappoint, expect a sharp pullback to $130–145; that would be the better entry. Size: 1% portfolio max — stock is near ATH, above consensus. Insider selling is notable: CEO sold $2.96M at $98.70 in March, CFO sold $4M at $134 in April. All below current price, but pattern of selling into strength.

exitPlan.label: Spread 1
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $175–180 (+6–9% from $165 strike) on earnings beat + target upgrades

exitPlan.label: Spread 2
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $185+ (+12%) on sustained AI ASIC revenue acceleration

companyOverview: Marvell Technology is a fabless semiconductor company specializing in data infrastructure — custom AI ASICs, 5G, networking, and storage silicon. It surged 67% in April 2026 on strong AI data-centre revenue and confirmation that custom ASIC production (for hyperscaler co-design programs) is scaling. Its 1.6T optical DSP and custom compute chips for AI training/inference workloads position it alongside Broadcom as a key beneficiary of the shift away from general-purpose NVIDIA GPUs toward custom silicon. The stock hit a new NASDAQ ATH of $170.84 on April 24, 2026 and is now at $164.74 — but has significantly outrun analyst consensus price targets ($120 average), meaning targets need to be revised upward for the current price to be justified.

catalysts: May 28 Q1 FY2027 earnings — critical: must confirm AI ASIC revenue is sustaining the $165+ valuation and trigger analyst target upgrades to $170–200 range
Custom ASIC co-design wins with hyperscalers (AWS, Google, Meta, Microsoft) — multi-year revenue visibility if 1–2 new programs confirmed
1.6T optical DSP chip ramp — next-gen data-centre interconnect demand driven by AI model scale-up
Bank of America bull thesis: 16x 2027 earnings vs. pricier AI chip peers — relatively undemanding if earnings delivery holds
Fabless model + TSMC N2/N3 process access — competitive advantage in power/performance per watt vs. Intel IDM
risks: Stock is ABOVE average analyst price target ($120) — the April 67% surge has outrun consensus; if May 28 earnings do not justify $165+, sharp reversion likely
May 28 earnings — IV crush guaranteed post-announcement; enter AFTER to avoid losing 30–40% of option premium to IV collapse
CEO and CFO both sold into strength at much lower prices — pattern of insider distribution, not accumulation
Custom ASIC dependency: Broadcom is the dominant custom ASIC player; MRVL's program wins are less disclosed and harder to verify
Any NVIDIA guidance change or hyperscaler capex pullback would reprice the entire AI chip sector
Only 0.19% insider ownership — executives treat this as salary, not investment conviction
dilution: Standard large-cap equity structure. No meaningful dilution risk. Share buyback program in place. Capital returns via dividends and buybacks.
